summary: The Docker-based reproduction is designed to demonstrate that the vulnerable java_import sink in JdbcDriver loads an arbitrary Java class whose static initializer executes attacker-controlled code inside the JRuby container.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the crafted ProofOfConcept Java class to be loaded via java_import at driver.rb:24, triggering its static initializer which creates /tmp/pwned.txt and /tmp/rce_successful as proof of arbitrary code execution.
actual_observed_result: PoC was not executed during this session; no runtime observation is available.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the existence and contents of /tmp/pwned.txt and /tmp/rce_successful inside the container. The presence of these files confirms the malicious class was loaded and its static initializer executed, distinguishing code execution from a generic class-not-found error.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker was not available in the audit environment to execute the reproduction container.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
